pitch: Set in a world where Weird West and Reality Competition coexist uneasily, our hero — a Bounty Hunter — must save... something. Probably a town.
title: Most Wanted: The Reckoning Round
synopsis: In the cursed boomtown of Gallows Gulch, the law has been replaced by a hit reality show, and grizzled bounty hunter Calloway "Deadeye" Pruitt is the reluctant frontrunner. To collect his largest bounty yet — a body-snatching outlaw who may also be a literal ghost — he must outlast eleven rivals on live television while a smiling host narrates his every blunder. Trouble is, Deadeye refuses to "play to camera," keeps shooting the confession-booth lantern, and the eliminated contestants don't go home — they get hanged. With the whole haunted town riding on the finale, he has one season to win, or the saloon gets foreclosed by demons.

pitch: Imagine if a Forensic Procedural show had a baby with a Cosmic Horror miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Ambitious Politician.
title: The Coroner's Constituent
synopsis: State Senator Marian Voss has built her career on closing cases—and now, with the governorship within reach, she's personally consulting on a string of impossible autopsies to look tough on crime. But every corpse on the slab shares the same impossible cause of death, the same geometry carved into the bone, and the same name whispered from inside the body bag: hers. As her poll numbers climb and her sanity erodes, Marian must decide whether the truth is worth a thing no ballot can contain. Some platforms were not meant to be built on this Earth.

pitch: Pitch: Cozy Whodunit x Revisionist Western. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Private Investigator.
title: High Noon at the Honeysuckle Inn
synopsis: When dapper city detective Cassius "Cash" Delacroix rides into the dust-choked frontier town of Marrow's Bend to solve a tea-cozy poisoning, he finds eleven suspects, one shared bathhouse, and a sheriff who smolders like a struck match. Every interrogation drips with sweat and longing; every alibi smells of bergamot and gunpowder. As the body count rises faster than the saloon-keeper's pulse, Cash must crack the case before sundown — or before he himself is undone by the widow in mourning lace. Dread has never been this damp.

pitch: Pitch: Revisionist Western x Psychological Horror. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Night-Shift Nurse.
title: The Laudanum Ward
synopsis: 1887, a frontier mining town's only hospital, lit by gaslight and bad intentions. Night-shift nurse Corah Quint works the graveyard rotation alone, dispensing morphine to men who keep dying of the same wound — a wound that wasn't there yesterday. When a charming, unkillable gunslinger checks himself into Bed 9 and starts whispering things only her dead husband knew, Corah realizes the ward isn't full of patients. It's full of her. Slow-burn, sweat-soaked, and quietly terrifying, it's a fever dream where every recovery is a relapse.

pitch: Picture a Food Doc setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Psychological Horror. Our reluctant hero: a Traveling Salesman.
title: The Sampler: A Taste of Dread
synopsis: Roy Tibble has sold premium artisanal salt to small towns for twenty-two years, and he's never met a customer he couldn't charm. But when his route lands him in Marender's Hollow — a fog-choked village obsessed with a "founder's stew" no one will describe — every doorbell he rings opens onto smiling neighbors who already know his name, his order, and his deepest fear. As the locals insist he taste "just one more spoonful," Roy realizes the documentary crew following him stopped blinking hours ago. A queasy, slow-burn dread about hospitality, hunger, and the unbearable politeness of people who want you for dinner.

pitch: It's Reality Competition meets Surreal Comedy... but the protagonist is a Disillusioned Priest.
title: Thou Shalt Not Get Voted Off
synopsis: Father Desmond Pellow lost his faith somewhere between the offering plate and the parish's leaking roof, so when a reality show called "Last Soul Standing" offers a $2 million prize, he signs up to compete against eleven strangers in a remote desert compound run by a smiling host who may or may not be God. The challenges are deeply unholy: assemble IKEA pews blindfolded, out-fast a competitive eater, deliver a sermon to a panel of judgmental flamingos. As the eliminations get weirder and the confessional booth starts talking back, Desmond must decide whether he's playing for the money, the redemption, or just the immunity idol shaped like a communion wafer. Faith is a game now. The tribe has spoken.

pitch: Cold open: a Reluctant Chosen One, alone, in the rain. The genre? Cyberpunk. The vibe? Unmistakably Martial Arts. Cue title card.
title: Neon Dojo: The Accidental Fist
synopsis: Dao Pham is a sleep-deprived noodle-cart vendor in the perpetually raining megacity of Neo-Saigon-9 who just wants his loan repayments to stop auto-deducting. Then a dying monk uploads the legendary "Iron Lotus" combat protocol straight into Dao's bootleg cybernetic spine, and now every black-market warlord in the city wants his nervous system. Problem: the firmware only activates when Dao is genuinely annoyed, which is constantly, and it crashes every time he sneezes. With a snarky holographic mentor nagging in his ear, Dao must master a martial art he never asked for before the corporate ninjas reformat him into a toaster.

pitch: Imagine if a Sports Doc show had a baby with a Slasher miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Grizzled Sea Captain.
title: Chum: A Death at Sea Documentary
synopsis: For forty years, Captain Augustus "Barnacle" Vane has guided his trawler crew to record-breaking marlin hauls — and a film crew is finally here to chronicle his legendary final season. But when deckhands start vanishing one foggy night and a hook-handed figure in a slicker begins picking off the team between interviews, the documentary becomes a fight for survival. Vane, who refuses to break character even while being chased, keeps narrating his own demise to the camera. The catch of the season may be the crew themselves.

pitch: Cold open: a Reclusive Author, alone, in the rain. The genre? Disaster. The vibe? Unmistakably Cold Case. Cue title card.
title: The Last Draft: Pressure Front
synopsis: Reclusive novelist Mordecai Vane hasn't left his cliffside cabin in eleven years — not since the night his collaborator vanished and the case went cold. Now a record-breaking superstorm is tearing the coastline apart, and the floodwaters are unearthing evidence everyone assumed the ocean had swallowed forever. Trapped with a detective who never closed the file and a manuscript that reads suspiciously like a confession, Mordecai has until the seawall fails to figure out who really did it. Spoiler: it might be in chapter twelve.

pitch: Picture a Workplace Romance setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Buddy Cop. Our reluctant hero: a Tech Billionaire.
title: Hostile Takeover: Partners in Crime
synopsis: Tech billionaire Dexter Vane has never filed his own expense report, let alone solved a corporate espionage case — so when his board assigns him a no-nonsense forensic auditor named Reyes to recover a stolen prototype, sparks fly in every wrong way. They argue over budgets, kick down server-room doors, and slow-dance at the company gala while wearing wires. He's a chaos-goblin who answers his own emails at 3 a.m.; she color-codes her ammunition. To save the company — and possibly each other — these mismatched partners must learn the deadliest skill of all: sharing a calendar.

pitch: It's First Contact meets Cozy Whodunit... but the protagonist is a Rookie Cop.
title: Welcome to Mudgepenny: A Cosmic Inconvenience
synopsis: Constable Daphne Crick has been on the Mudgepenny village force for exactly nine days, and her biggest case so far was a stolen prize marrow. Then a 400-foot luminous starship parks itself in the duck pond and a beloved jam-judge turns up dead at the harvest fête. Now Daphne must solve a locked-greenhouse murder while interviewing seven gossiping pensioners and one polite, eight-eyed alien ambassador who insists it "comes in peace but does enjoy a good clue." With first contact and tea cooling fast, she has until the church bells ring to name the killer — terrestrial or otherwise.

pitch: A Mob Enforcer stumbles into a Indie Folk Musical conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Acid Western.
title: The Ballad of Knuckles McGraw
synopsis: Vincent "Knuckles" McGraw breaks kneecaps for the Genovetti family until a botched shakedown at a folk open-mic curses him to communicate only through plaintive acoustic ballads. To lift the hex, he must wander a sun-blasted, mescaline-warped frontier where every gunfight is settled by harmony and every clue is hidden in a murder ballad's third verse. Pursued by a banjo-wielding desert prophet and his own off-key conscience, Knuckles learns the only way out is to finger-pick the truth. It's a tender, hallucinatory showdown between a hitman and his feelings.

pitch: Pitch: Coming-of-Age x Conspiracy Doc. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Cult Leader.
title: The Anointed Summer of Brayden Voss
synopsis: At seventeen, Brayden Voss thinks he's just charismatic — until a documentary crew arrives to film the backyard "wellness collective" he accidentally founded after a viral journaling post. Equal parts hormonal disaster and reluctant messiah, he must navigate first love, his learner's permit, and the seventeen adults now sleeping in his mom's garage who believe he can decode the moon. As the doc crew pushes for darker footage and a rival cult slides into his DMs, Brayden discovers that growing up and going full apocalypse-prophet feel disturbingly similar. Narrated entirely in breathy, ominous voiceover.

pitch: Cold open: a Reclusive Author, alone, in the rain. The genre? Creature Feature. The vibe? Unmistakably Buddy Comedy. Cue title card.
title: My Beast Friend
synopsis: Agoraphobic horror novelist Marvin Pell hasn't left his cabin in eleven years — until a nine-foot lake creature crashes through his skylight, reads his unfinished manuscript, and declares it "honestly kind of mid." Now bound together by a curse neither understands, the prickly recluse and his unnervingly chatty amphibian roommate must survive a town of torch-wielding locals, a deadline-obsessed editor, and the horrifying ordeal of going outside. Marvin needs the monster's help to finish his book. The monster just needs a best friend. Somebody's getting eaten, and somebody's getting closure.

pitch: Cold open: a Disillusioned Priest, alone, in the rain. The genre? Mythic Retelling. The vibe? Unmistakably Cosmic Horror. Cue title card.
title: The Gospel of the Drowning Stars
synopsis: Father Elias Crane lost his faith somewhere between the third funeral and the second whiskey — until the saints in his cathedral's stained glass start weeping black seawater and whispering coordinates. Tasked with translating an "ancient miracle" beneath his flooded parish, Elias discovers the holy texts were never about God, but about Something older, wetter, and dreaming just offshore. Now the only lapsed priest in a fishing town must decide whether to perform the rites that wake it — or the heresy that might keep it sleeping. Faith was the easy part. Salvation has tentacles.

pitch: Three-act structure: Mockumentary in act one, Cozy Fantasy by act three, and a Struggling Artist sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
title: Mostly Magic: A Documentary
synopsis: Failed mural painter Dennis Crubbe agrees to let a film crew document his "comeback," which mostly involves crying into a microwave lasagna and overdrawing his rent. Then a tiny, judgmental hearth spirit named Pim moves into his radiator and starts critiquing his color choices — and slowly fixing his life with cottage magic, enchanted soup, and aggressively cozy houseplants. As the documentary lens drifts from "broke loser" to "reluctant fairy-tale wizard," Dennis must decide whether he's ready to be happy on camera. Warm, deadpan, and suspiciously emotional about a radiator gremlin.

pitch: Three-act structure: Paranormal Romance in act one, Cold Case by act three, and a Disillusioned Priest sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
title: Cold Case of the Heart
synopsis: Father Emil Vance lost his faith somewhere between funeral number four hundred and the discovery that his rectory's microwave is the only thing that loves him back. Then a heartbreakingly polite ghost named Lucinda starts haunting his confessional, asking him to solve her 1987 murder — and, awkwardly, to fall in love with her while he's at it. As Emil reopens the coldest case in the diocese, he must weigh decades-old evidence, an unholy crush, and the very real possibility he's just a sad man talking to a draft. Romance has never been this overdue, or this dead.

pitch: Imagine if a Urban Fantasy show had a baby with a Satire miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Undertaker.
title: Grave Concerns: A Modern Embalming
synopsis: Mortimer Vane runs the last family-owned funeral home in a city where the dead won't shut up — literally, since the recently deceased now file complaints, demand five-star burials, and leave online reviews. When a venture-backed startup launches "AfterLyfe," a subscription service that resurrects the dead as compliant brand ambassadors, Mortimer becomes the only undertaker willing to let people actually stay buried. Armed with an enchanted embalming kit and a deeply unfashionable belief in dignity, he wages a polite, paperwork-heavy war against eternal life as a customer-loyalty program. Death, it turns out, has a marketing department.

pitch: Set in a world where Spy Thriller and Locked Room coexist uneasily, our hero — a Stage Magician — must save... something. Probably a town.
title: The Vanishing Cabinet Protocol
synopsis: Washed-up Vegas illusionist Cornelius "The Astonishing" Vale gets handed the worst gig of his career: a foreign intelligence service has sealed the only access panel to a nuclear failsafe inside a vault that locks from the inside, and the lone witness vanished mid-trick. With his sequined assistant doubling as a reluctant MI6 liaison and a deck of trick cards as his only weapon, Cornelius has 90 minutes to misdirect a room full of armed spies, solve who locked it, and pick a deadbolt with a dove. The catch: nobody left the room, and one of them isn't real. Prepare to be deceived — for your country.

pitch: It's Alien Invasion meets Mockumentary... but the protagonist is a Cult Leader.
title: Honk If You Believe: The Glorbax Testimonies
synopsis: A documentary crew follows Brother Dale Pucket, charismatic leader of the Sedona-based Order of Eternal Upward Mobility, as he insists the mothership hovering over his compound is here specifically to collect him and his $400 starter robes. When the actual Glorbax invasion fleet begins vaporizing the suburbs, Dale reframes it as a "membership drive" and frantically sells parking spots in the apocalypse. The aliens, baffled, request a meeting with management. It's a faith-based feature about commitment, branding, and not reading the fine print on first contact.

pitch: It's Post-Apocalyptic meets Slasher... but the protagonist is a Night-Shift Nurse.
title: Code Blue: The Last Shift
synopsis: Twelve years after the bombs fell, Marlene Voss runs the night shift at the last functioning hospital on Earth — a flickering wing powered by a bicycle generator and sheer spite. When a masked figure in a gas mask and surgical scrubs starts "discharging" survivors one by one, Marlene must protect her six remaining patients with nothing but a defibrillator, a clipboard, and a strict no-running-in-the-hallways policy. She hasn't slept since the apocalypse and she is NOT about to start a paperwork backlog now. The Reaper keeps regular hours; so does she.

pitch: Imagine if a Fairy Tale show had a baby with a Body Horror miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Burned-Out Surgeon.
title: The Grimm Ward: A Surgeon's Cure
synopsis: Dr. Margit Vasco hasn't slept in nine years and has stopped believing fairy tales can hurt you — until a coughing child in her ER hacks up a living, blinking rosebud. Soon her patients are sprouting briar-thorn ribs, weeping golden teeth, and growing extra thumbs that whistle lullabies. Margit, who medicates exhaustion with vending-machine coffee and dark sarcasm, realizes the curse is contagious and she's Patient Zero of a "happily ever after" rewriting her own anatomy. To break the spell she must operate on herself before the storybook finishes the ending. Some cures are worse than the fairy tale.

pitch: Pitch: Gothic Horror x Jukebox Musical. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Aging Movie Star.
title: Sunset Boulevard of the Damned
synopsis: Faded screen siren Vesper Lamont hasn't left her crumbling Hollywood mansion in forty years — and neither have the studio executives she buried in the rose garden. When a young location scout reawakens the house, Vesper discovers her glory days return whenever she sings, draining decades off everyone who joins the chorus. Now she's staging the deadliest comeback in history: a full-throated jukebox spectacular powered by the souls of her audience. The encore is eternal. The reviews are murderous.

pitch: It's Military Action meets Tragic Musical... but the protagonist is a Con Artist.
title: Desert Storm: A Ballad of Lies
synopsis: Corporal "Smiling" Vance Mireles never enlisted — he forged the papers, stole the rank, and conned an entire forward operating base into believing he's a decorated war hero. The trouble is, the only way he can rally his doomed platoon out of an ambush is by singing — and every soaring power-ballad he belts reveals one more devastating lie about who he really is. As the artillery falls in slow-motion and the strings swell, Vance must choose between the magnificent fraud that made him beloved and the truth that could save the men who'd die for him. It's a heartbreaking, brass-and-tears musical about a man whose finest performance is also his last.

pitch: Cold open: a Reclusive Author, alone, in the rain. The genre? Cold Case. The vibe? Unmistakably Sports Doc. Cue title card.
title: The Ghostwriter's Last Inning
synopsis: Reclusive novelist Sol Ardway hasn't left his rain-soaked cabin in nineteen years — not since the night his ghostwritten sports memoir went to print and its subject, a beloved minor-league pitcher, vanished without a trace. Now a fame-hungry documentary crew has talked their way onto his porch, convinced the unsolved disappearance is buried somewhere in Sol's unpublished final chapter. As cold-case clues collide with slow-motion replays and confessional talking-heads, Sol must decide whether the perfect ending he invented was also the perfect alibi. Some stories were never meant to be edited.

pitch: Picture a Spaghetti Western setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Hard Sci-Fi. Our reluctant hero: a Rookie Cop.
title: High Noon at Heat Death
synopsis: Newly badged Deputy Wren Calloway patrols Perdition Flats, a dust-choked frontier town orbiting a dying red dwarf, where every duel is settled not by who draws fastest but by who correctly calculates relativistic bullet drift first. When a notorious outlaw arrives claiming he can violate the second law of thermodynamics, Wren — who faints at the sight of entropy — must master orbital mechanics, peer review, and the quick-draw before sundown takes 11.7 minutes to arrive. The townsfolk debate it in a saloon that doubles as a cryogenics lab. It's the slowest fastest gun in the galaxy.

pitch: Pitch: Heist x Legal Drama. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Small-Town Sheriff.
title: Probable Cause: The Vault of Sweetwater
synopsis: Sheriff Delilah "Del" Caruthers runs the laziest county in Texas — until the slickest crew in the Southwest rolls into Sweetwater to crack the First Federal vault, and their ringleader turns out to be the defense attorney she once kissed at law school and never got over. When the heist goes wrong and bodies need explaining, Del has to prosecute the case AND chase the loot, all while pretending her courtroom voice doesn't go husky every time she objects. It's slow-burn dread with sweat on the upper lip. Justice has never been this damp.

pitch: Pitch: High Fantasy x Heist. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Fortune Teller.
title: The Last Card: A Prophecy of Larceny
synopsis: Madame Vesryn, a velvet-draped seer who can read any future except her own, owes a blood-debt to the Archmage of Thornhold — and his vault holds the one relic that could rewrite her fate. So she assembles a crew: a sweat-slick lockpicking paladin, an enchantress who flirts as fluently as she lies, and a goblin who keeps proposing. The catch: every tarot pull warns that one of them dies tonight, and Vesryn refuses to ask which. Equal parts doom, longing, and grand-larceny, it's a candlelit con where destiny is just another mark to be robbed.

pitch: Picture a Cold Case setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Food Doc. Our reluctant hero: a Burned-Out Surgeon.
title: Cold Cuts: A Case Gone Cold
synopsis: Dr. Vance Marlow hasn't held a scalpel since the surgery that ended his career — now he holds a tasting spoon. Lured out of retirement to crack a 22-year-old unsolved murder, he treats the frozen evidence like a Michelin tasting menu, lovingly narrating each blood-spatter pattern and decomposing clue in hushed, reverent foodie tones. As suspects "plate" their alibis and a rival detective sears the truth tableside, Vance discovers the killer left a signature as distinctive as a reduction. Equal parts forensic thriller and prestige cooking show, justice has never looked so deliciously overplated.

pitch: Three-act structure: Hardboiled Detective in act one, Rock Opera by act three, and a Disgraced Detective sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
title: Dead Man's Reverb
synopsis: Detective Frankie "Knuckles" Malone got bounced off the force for tampering with evidence — and also for sobbing during interrogations. Now a washed-up gumshoe haunting a rain-soaked city, he stumbles onto a murder that can only be solved by feeling things, loudly, in four-part harmony. As the case cracks open, so does Frankie: the noir voiceover gives way to power ballads, the precinct becomes a strobe-lit arena, and his microwave lasagna becomes a recurring duet partner. To catch the killer, he must do the one thing a hardboiled man fears most — hit the high note.

pitch: Logline: When a Con Artist discovers their quiet life is actually a Psychological Drama, they must master the genre conventions of Gothic Horror to survive.
title: The Marks of Blackthorn Manor
synopsis: Slick grifter Ezra Voss has scammed his way into a cushy "inheritance" at a fog-drowned country estate, certain the grieving widow is his easiest mark yet. But the candles relight themselves, the portraits keep score, and the house seems to know every lie he's ever told. As Ezra's confidence games unravel into something far more intimate — and the manor begins billing him interest on his sins — he realizes the only con left is convincing a haunted house he's worth keeping alive. To survive the night, the world's best liar must do the one thing he never has: mean it.

pitch: Three-act structure: Marriage of Convenience in act one, Spy Thriller by act three, and a Ambitious Politician sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
title: Till Cover Do Us Part
synopsis: Senator-hopeful Diane Plimpton-Vasquez needs a spotless family image; freelance asset Marcus "Three Passports" Vane needs a legal address that isn't a safehouse. Their fake marriage is a flawless poll-booster — until her opponent turns out to run an arms cartel and Marcus's "consulting job" turns out to be assassinating it. Now Diane must survive a gala shootout, a leaked sex tape that's actually a dead-drop, and the crushing realization that the only man who's ever remembered her birthday does it because it's also a detonation code. Between debates she cries into a microwave lasagna. He finds it oddly endearing.

pitch: Logline: When a Stage Magician discovers their quiet life is actually a True Crime, they must master the genre conventions of Spy Thriller to survive.
title: Sleight of Hand: The Disappearing Asset
synopsis: Mild-mannered illusionist Roland "The Astonishing" Beck thinks his biggest secret is how he palms a half-dollar. Then a leaked surveillance tape reveals his stage assistant has been laundering state secrets through the vanishing-dove act for a decade — and Roland's name is on every dead drop. With a reluctant documentary crew filming his every move and three intelligence agencies certain he's the most dangerous double agent alive, Roland must weaponize misdirection, escape artistry, and one extremely real rabbit to clear his name. Turns out "now you see me" is excellent tradecraft.

pitch: A Corporate Whistleblower stumbles into a Body Horror conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Noir.

synopsis: Mid-level compliance analyst Dale Voss thought he was just reporting expense fraud — until he found the memo that explains why everyone in Accounts Payable is growing a second mouth. Now the only person who believes him is the chain-smoking ghost of his own conscience, and the only way out is to follow the oldest rule in the book: trust no one, especially the guy in the mirror who keeps shedding. As Synthrex Corporation "onboards" its employees into something wetter, Dale chases a paper trail that bleeds. The truth is in the building. So is whatever the building is becoming.

pitch: Set in a world where First Contact and Cosmic Horror coexist uneasily, our hero — a Bounty Hunter — must save... something. Probably a town.
title: The Skip-Trace of Mawr County
synopsis: Disgraced bounty hunter Cassidy "Coyote" Reyes tracks fugitives for a living, until her latest bounty turns out to be the only human who knows that the friendly aliens who landed in Mawr County, Nebraska, are merely the larval stage of something with too many angles to look at directly. Now her quarry is the one warning her to run. As the townsfolk start humming the same impossible note in their sleep, Coyote must decide whether to collect her paycheck or save the seven thousand people who keep insisting everything is fine. Her quirks: she narrates her own arrests, and she cannot stop sketching the geometry that is slowly unmaking her mind.

pitch: Picture a Sports Doc setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Found Footage. Our reluctant hero: a Single Parent.
title: Little League of the Damned
synopsis: Divorced dad Greg Pernicki just wanted to film his daughter's championship Little League season for the grandparents. But somewhere between the third inning and the concession stand, the camcorder starts catching things no scoreboard can explain — a base coach who never blinks, dugout chants in a language nobody taught the kids, and an umpire who calls strikes before the pitch is thrown. As parents vanish one tailgate at a time, Greg keeps rolling, because the only thing scarier than the thing in left field is missing Lily's first home run. Shot entirely on one increasingly cursed handheld camera.

pitch: Cold open: a Night-Shift Nurse, alone, in the rain. The genre? Dance Musical. The vibe? Unmistakably Sports Doc. Cue title card.
title: Code Blue: The Comeback Routine
synopsis: Marisol Vega works the 3 a.m. shift on the cardiac floor, where she's secretly choreographed an entire jazz-funk number around the rhythm of the heart monitors. When a rival hospital challenges her ward to the National Defibrillator Dance-Off, a deadpan documentary crew embeds with the team to capture every grueling rehearsal, every sequined scrub, every blistered clog. Part triumphant musical, part sweat-soaked sports doc, it follows one exhausted nurse chasing glory between vitals checks. Will she flatline — or bring the house down before sunrise?

pitch: Logline: When a Lighthouse Keeper discovers their quiet life is actually a Reality Competition, they must master the genre conventions of Martial Arts to survive.
title: Last One Standing: The Lighthouse Gauntlet
synopsis: Bartholomew "Barty" Quill has tended the Stormgrave Lighthouse alone for nineteen peaceful years — until a foghorn blares, stadium lights ignite the cliffs, and a host announces he's been the unwitting star of "Last One Standing," the world's deadliest reality competition. Now ninety-three eliminated contestants want his immunity lantern, and the only way off the island is to win. Armed with a mop, a thermos of chowder, and martial arts he absorbed from late-night VHS tapes, mild-mannered Barty must roundhouse his way to a finale. The cameras never blink. Neither can he.

pitch: Pitch: Indie Folk Musical x Buddy Cop. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Fortune Teller.
title: Crystal Ball and Chain
synopsis: Detective Roma Velasquez can see exactly how everyone dies — except her insufferably handsome new partner, Detective Cole Briggs, whose future is a humming blank she finds infuriatingly, dangerously attractive. Forced to solve a string of murders she keeps predicting one verse too late, the pair process every clue, hunch, and unbearable longing in fingerpicked acoustic ballads. As a hooded killer closes in, Roma must decide whether to trust her tarot or the man whose death she cannot foresee. It's tender. It's sweaty. It's harmonizing about probable cause.

pitch: A Ex-Spy stumbles into a Period Piece conspiracy that can only be solved using the rules of Tragic Musical.
title: The Spy Who Sang Too Late
synopsis: Vienna, 1911. Retired imperial spymaster Auguste Vereaux has sworn off ciphers for a quiet life conducting the opera house orchestra. But when a coded assassination plot surfaces hidden inside an aria, Auguste discovers the conspiracy obeys the iron laws of tragic musical theater: every secret must be confessed in song, every villain gets exactly one heartbreaking solo, and no one survives the third act finale. To save the Archduke, Auguste must out-warble a baritone double agent — knowing that whoever hits the final high note dies for it.

title: The Velvet Congregation
synopsis: Disgraced grifter Dorian Vane fakes his way into the Order of the Crimson Lamb — a candlelit hilltop commune of beautiful zealots — planning to swindle their tithe-rich coffers and slip out by dawn. But the documentary crew he hired as a cover starts capturing things their lenses shouldn't: doors that breathe, a charismatic prophetess who hasn't aged since 1612, and devotees who whisper Dorian's real name in their sleep. The longer he cons them, the more the manor seems to be conning him. By the third confessional interview, he can't tell if he's running the scam — or starring in the ritual.

pitch: Pitch: Mockumentary x Gothic Horror. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Night-Shift Nurse.
title: The Graveyard Shift: A Bedside Manor
synopsis: A scrappy documentary crew thinks they're filming a feel-good piece about chronically understaffed Blackmoor Sanatorium — until they meet Sister Imelda Vane, the night nurse who hasn't aged since 1887 and insists the patients "check out, but they never leave." Between deadpan talking-head interviews and lingering candlelit glances, the crew slowly realizes the call buttons ring from empty beds and Imelda's "smoke break" is just her exhaling at a portrait of a dead duke. It's all very professional. It's also deeply, unsettlingly horny. As the sound guy starts sleepwalking toward the morgue, the crew must decide: get the Emmy, or get out alive.

pitch: Pitch: Buddy Cop x Workplace Comedy. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Disgraced Athlete.
title: Personal Foul: Badge of Dishonor
synopsis: Disgraced ex-quarterback Roy "Hot Hands" Delgado — banned from the league for the infamous "fumble that broke a city's heart" — is sentenced to community service as a civilian liaison at the 14th Precinct's Records Department. Partnered with Detective Anita Vance, a sleep-deprived workaholic who communicates exclusively in passive-aggressive sticky notes, Roy must solve a corruption case while filing overtime forms in triplicate. The tension is professional. The tension is also, regrettably, not just professional. Between stakeouts and surprise HR seminars, they'll learn that justice is a team sport and the copier is jammed again.

pitch: Pitch: Spy Thriller x Mockumentary. Tone: dread, but make it horny. Lead: a Idealistic Lawyer.
title: Sworn In: A Field Study in Treason
synopsis: Idealistic young attorney Marisol "Mari" Vega thinks she's been hired to fight for the little guy — until a documentary crew, hired by an agency that doesn't officially exist, follows her through her first week at a law firm that's actually a CIA front. As she earnestly files pro bono motions, she keeps accidentally extracting state secrets, sleeping with at least two double agents, and signing what may or may not be a confession. The handheld interviews get sweatier and the briefcase gets heavier. Justice is blind, but the surveillance cameras are not.

pitch: Imagine if a Revisionist Western show had a baby with a Gothic Horror miniseries, and that baby grew up to follow a Exiled Royal.
title: The Pale Crown of Buzzard Creek
synopsis: Deposed boy-king Aldric Vane flees a coup with nothing but a tarnished crown, a borrowed Stetson, and a cough that won't quit. He rides into Buzzard Creek hoping to vanish — but the town's saloon is run by something centuries old that remembers what royal blood tastes like. As the gunslingers turn gray and the church bell tolls on its own, Aldric must learn to draw a revolver faster than he ever learned to bow. There are no heroes out here. Only a king, a curse, and a very thirsty congregation.

pitch: Picture a Car-Chase Thriller setting, but everyone behaves like they're in a Marriage of Convenience. Our reluctant hero: a Ex-Spy.
title: Till Pursuit Do Us Part
synopsis: Burned-out ex-spy Dex Calloway needs a fake marriage to slip past a border checkpoint with a stolen ledger — so he weds his getaway driver, Nadia, in a drive-thru chapel doing 60mph. Now the most dangerous cartel in three countries is in the rearview, but the real crisis is that Nadia keeps "accidentally" rear-ending the agency that's tailing them, and Dex forgot their anniversary (it was Tuesday). They must dodge missiles, debate whose name goes on the joint passport, and discover that a marriage of convenience comes with very inconvenient feelings. Buckle up — and apologize.

pitch: Three-act structure: Period Romance in act one, Dark Comedy by act three, and a Lighthouse Keeper sobbing into a microwave dinner the whole time.
title: A Lamp for the Drowning Heart
synopsis: In 1887 on a storm-battered Cornish rock, lonesome lighthouse keeper Bartholomew Quill falls desperately in love with Cordelia, a shipwreck survivor he nurses back to health — until he realizes she keeps surviving shipwrecks, suspiciously, and that several of her previous "fiancés" are now lighthouse-shaped. As the romance curdles into a politely homicidal battle of wits, Bartholomew defends his beloved beacon, his dignity, and his nightly microwaved kipper supper. It's tender. It's deranged. Someone gets fed to a foghorn.

pitch: Cold open: a Undertaker, alone, in the rain. The genre? Post-Apocalyptic. The vibe? Unmistakably Found Footage. Cue title card.
title: The Last Embalmer: Tape 7
synopsis: Mort Kessler ran the only funeral home left standing after the world ended, and he refuses to bury anyone without proper paperwork — even when civilization itself is the corpse. Armed with a dying camcorder and an unshakable belief in "dignity for the deceased," he documents his rounds through the ash-choked ruins, narrating each body's eulogy to a microphone nobody will ever hear. But the dead in the wasteland have stopped staying buried, and Mort's footage captures something following him home. This is the tape they found in his hearse. It is the only thing that survived.

pitch: Set in a world where Body Horror and Dance Musical coexist uneasily, our hero — a Mob Enforcer — must save... something. Probably a town.
title: Goodfella Pirouette: A Mob in Pieces
synopsis: Vinnie "The Hinge" Carbone is the most feared knee-breaker in Cannelloni City — until a cursed protection racket leaves him with a body that won't stop dancing. Every time the music swells, his joints reassemble into jazz hands, his ribs unfold into spirit fingers, and his boss's enemies are choreographed to death. Now Vinnie must out-dance a rival family of contortionist body-snatchers before the whole borough peels itself into a chorus line. It's a horror musical where the only way to survive is to nail the landing.
